title: Indian Badminton Squad for Asian Games 2026 Announced
indian_angle: This news details the full Indian contingent, including top stars like Sindhu and Satwik-Chirag, who will represent the nation at the prestigious Asian Games.
summary: The Badminton Association of India (BAI) has finalized the Indian badminton squad for the 20th Asian Games 2026, featuring prominent players like PV Sindhu, Lakshya Sen, and the men's doubles pair of Satwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty, based on recent performances and BWF rankings.

title: Tanvi Sharma's Game Style: Saina Nehwal or PV Sindhu?
indian_angle: This feature provides an in-depth analysis of a promising young Indian shuttler, offering insights into her potential trajectory in Indian badminton.
summary: An analytical piece explores the evolving game of young Indian shuttler Tanvi Sharma, drawing comparisons between her playing style and those of legendary Indian players Saina Nehwal and PV Sindhu, particularly after her performance at the Australian Open Super 500.

title: Grassroot Coaches Development Program Kicks Off in Guwahati & Raipur
indian_angle: This program is crucial for nurturing future badminton talent across India by enhancing the skills of grassroots coaches.
summary: The Badminton Association of India (BAI) has issued a circular for a Coaches Development Program at the National Centres of Excellence in Guwahati and Raipur, running from June 12th to 21st, 2026, focusing on grassroots-level coaching.

title: Coach Tan Kim Her's Strategy for Hari-Arjun Doubles Pair
indian_angle: This article highlights the strategic coaching behind another Indian doubles pair, following the success of Satwik-Chirag, indicating depth in Indian doubles talent development.
summary: A feature article details how renowned doubles coach Tan Kim Her strategically paired up Hariharan Amsakarunan and Arjun M.R., aiming to replicate the success he achieved with India's top men's doubles duo, Satwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty.

title: India Submits EOI for BWF World Championships 2026
indian_angle: India's expression of interest to host the BWF World Championships signifies its growing prominence in global badminton and potential for a major home event.
summary: The Badminton Association of India (BAI) has submitted an Expression of Interest (EOI) to host the prestigious BWF World Championships in 2026, signaling India's ambition to bring top-tier international badminton events to the country.

title: New 3x15 Scoring System for Junior Domestic Tournaments
indian_angle: This rule change impacts the development and competitive experience of young Indian shuttlers in national tournaments.
summary: The Badminton Association of India (BAI) has announced the implementation of a 3x15 scoring system for all domestic ranking tournaments and National Championships across the Under-13, Under-15, and Under-17 age categories, starting from the 2026-27 season.
